threats.title: Anonymous access via a public bucket or ACL
threats.description: A public-read policy or a permissive ACL lets anyone list or fetch objects without authenticating.

threats.title: Unrestricted upload or object overwrite
threats.description: Writable-by-untrusted or content-unvalidated uploads let an attacker plant or replace objects (including served content).

threats.title: Objects readable or unencrypted at rest
threats.description: Public-read exposure or missing server-side encryption leaks stored objects.

threats.title: Bucket policy grants broader access than intended
threats.description: An over-broad principal or action in the policy extends access beyond what the workload needs.
